subject:
Re: Tomogram CTF estimation / Reconstruction of CTF corrected
 tomograms
body:
Update:

Julia Mahamid let me know that she found the "Start Processing" button 
doesn't seem to work properly and that I should try to go into the "Fourier 
Space" tab and press "Process only this item's CTF". Doing this got me a 
little further (thanks again Julia!).

The "Fourier Space" tab shows my tilt series, the GUI does not show the 
correct dose here but checking in the corresponding tomostar file shows the 
correct dose values. If I press "Process only this items CTF" it starts to 
process (yay!) but fairly quickly spits out an error and some possible 
fixes - it suggests read/write permissions, incorrect gain reference 
dimensions or too low defocus to have more than one peak in the searched 
CTF range.

The error itself appears to indicate an error in the actual estimation 
process rather than permissions or gain reference problems - 
System.Exception: Bad score.
   at 
Warp.TiltSeries.<>c__DisplayClass97_5.<ProcessCTFSimultaneous>b__17(Double[] 
input) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\TiltSeries.cs:line 1726
   at 
Warp.TiltSeries.<>c__DisplayClass97_5.<ProcessCTFSimultaneous>b__18(Double[] 
input) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\TiltSeries.cs:line 1742
   at 
Accord.Math.Optimization.NonlinearObjectiveFunction.CheckGradient(Func`2 
value, Double[] probe)
   at Accord.Math.Optimization.BaseGradientOptimizationMethod.Maximize()
   at Warp.TiltSeries.ProcessCTFSimultaneous(ProcessingOptionsMovieCTF 
options) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\TiltSeries.cs:line 1768
   at WarpWorker.Program.Main(String[] args) in 
D:\Dev\warp2\WarpWorker\Program.cs:line 206

I don't think my defocus is too low to have more than one peak in the 
searched CTF range (40-10A - corresponds to the start of the first peak 
down to where I can reliably get signal in images up to 15 degrees or 
so)... some ctf fits for the individual tilt images at 0 and 15 degrees are 
attached to this message. I get the same error for every tilt series 
unfortunately.

The "Start Processing" button not working seemed to be due to the 
tilt-series somehow becoming deselected - they cannot be easily reselected 
from the overview tab but can be reselected from the fourier space tab - 
this makes the start processing button work.
Running with the "Start Processing" button I get the following WarpWorker 
output

Running on GPU #0 (5006 MB free) through 
WarpWorkere1dd2761-6ec7-4287-bc95-d7e4706dfa4f

Received "SetHeaderlessParams", with 3 arguments, for GPU #0, 5006 MB free
Set headerless parameters to 3710, 3838, 0, int16
0.000

Received "LoadGainRef", with 4 arguments, for GPU #0, 5006 MB free
Loaded gain reference: , False, False, False
0.000

Received "TomoProcessCTF", with 2 arguments, for GPU #0, 5006 MB free
System.Exception: Bad score.
   at 
Warp.TiltSeries.<>c__DisplayClass97_5.<ProcessCTFSimultaneous>b__17(Double[] 
input) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\TiltSeries.cs:line 1726
   at 
Warp.TiltSeries.<>c__DisplayClass97_5.<ProcessCTFSimultaneous>b__18(Double[] 
input) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\TiltSeries.cs:line 1742
   at 
Accord.Math.Optimization.NonlinearObjectiveFunction.CheckGradient(Func`2 
value, Double[] probe)
   at Accord.Math.Optimization.BaseGradientOptimizationMethod.Maximize()
   at Warp.TiltSeries.ProcessCTFSimultaneous(ProcessingOptionsMovieCTF 
options) in D:\Dev\warp2\WarpLib\TiltSeries.cs:line 1768
   at WarpWorker.Program.Main(String[] args) in 
D:\Dev\warp2\WarpWorker\Program.cs:line 206

Another note, I get the same error when running on only a subset of tilt 
images in a tilt series (0-15degrees)

Any tips? 

Thanks,

Alister

On Monday, 2 December 2019 18:16:48 UTC+1, Alister Burt wrote:
>
> It seems like this may be a memory issue: 
> https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/warp-em/vwpQHNeji9s 
>
> Although because it hangs and doesn't spit out any error I'd like some 
> confirmation that I'm trying the right sort of thing if anyone has more 
> experience!
>
> On Monday, 2 December 2019 18:10:09 UTC+1, Alister Burt wrote:
>>
>> Hi all,
>>
>>
>> I’ve used warp for preprocessing of some movies from tilt-series. Used 
>> warp with the mdoc files to generate my stacks for IMOD, I then align these 
>> in IMOD. I import the aligned stacks back into WARP, it successfully 
>> imports the tilt-series (they come up as deselected in the overview tab) 
>> but I'm having trouble getting it to estimate the CTF (and thus reconstruct 
>> the tomogram)
>>
>>  
>>
>> I'm looking to generate ctf corrected tomograms - what I imagine to be 
>> the process is:
>>
>>    - Use the "import tilt series from IMOD" dialogue to import tilt 
>>    series and their alignments
>>    - Set parameters for unbinned tomogram size
>>    - Make sure pixel size X/Y is set properly
>>    - Click the CTF tickbox with appropriate CTF estimation parameters
>>    - Hit “Start Processing” to go through the thorough tomogram CTF 
>>    estimation procedure as described in the paper and save all necessary 
>>    metadata
>>    - Use the “Reconstruct Full Tomograms” dialogue to reconstruct 
>>    tomograms with the CTF corrected at a given pixel size etc etc
>>
>>
>> I import the tilt series into warp - this creates tomostar files in my 
>> frames folder which seem to be filled with the right information. 
>> _wrpMovieName #1, _wrpAngleTilt #2, _wrpAxisAngle #3, _wrpAxisOffsetX #4, 
>> _wrpAxisOffsetY #5, _wrpDose #6
>>
>> When I click "Start Processing" with CTF parameters set appropriately and 
>> without the gain correction step checked, nothing seems to happen.
>>
>> I've attached my parameter setup to this message. 
>>
>> In the WarpWorker.exe box which pops up I have the following...
>> Running on GPU #0 (5006 MB free) through 
>> WarpWorker632fd553-8169-4190-9c51-7a2c4a8d707a
>>
>> Received "SetHeaderlessParams", with 3 arguments, for GPU #0, 5006 MB free
>> Set headerless parameters to 3710, 3838, 0, int16
>> 0.000
>>
>> Received "LoadGainRef", with 4 arguments, for GPU #0, 5006 MB free
>> Loaded gain reference: , False, False, False
>> 0.000
>>
>> It just seems to hang here indefinitely.
>>
>> This part isn't described in the user guides yet so I'm sure there's 
>> probably something simple I'm doing wrong - has anybody managed to get past 
>> here and has some tips for me?
>>
>> Thanks in advance,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>
